title: Revealing the truth with surface metrics: measurement of unknown, multi-scale soft surfaces
description: Established a rigorous framework using laser scanning confocal microscopy to accurately measure and characterize the complex, multi-scale textures of soft solids. This provides a roadmap to revealing true cut surface topographies.

title: Fractography: Multiscale, quantitative isnights into soft material failure.
description: Fractured surfaces provides a window into the dynamics of failure. This work uses the developed fractographic tool to extract quantitative metrics from fractured surfaces to understand the physics of failure. Using experimental and computational explanations, a new framework about the failure of soft solids is explored.

title: Cutting of highly deformable tough materials.
description: Unlike soft elastomers like commonly used silicones, some materials (highly entangled hydrogels and tougher silicones) possess both high toughness and undergo large deformations. This work explores the cutting mechanics of such materials.

title: Exploring fracture-friction coupling with needle insertion
description: Investigating how fracture and friction are fundamentally coupled during needle insertion into soft tissues. This work explores how newly fractured surfaces create frictional interfaces that feed back into mechanical response—a relationship that is critical for surgical needle design and robotic insertion control.

title: Protorheology & Mullins effect
description: Course project (TAM 534): using protorheology to demonstrate Mullins damage in solid elastomers.

title: Constitutive modeling of soft materials
description: Course project (TAM 598): constitutive models to characterize highly deformable soft materials and connect to fracture behavior.

title: Fetal membrane mechanics review
description: Review (ME 482) on mechanical properties of the fetal membrane in the context of rupture.

title: Digital Scholars ECE camp
description: Summer 2024 instructor for DPI Chicago / UIUC: curriculum, Arduino robot capstone, and mentoring for high school participants.
